title: Commerce-Raiding Prey
note: What the crew find when they pick a hunting ground and look for prey. REPLACES generic encounter when raiding. Stat the hull from ships-of-the-reach.md; doctrine = disable, don't destroy. Heat/Standing consequences via subsystems.md.

entries.value: Free Trader (Type A, ~200t) — lone hauler, light or no escort; the bread-and-butter prize
Far Trader (Type A2, ~200t) — faster, jump-2, may run rather than fight
Subsidized Merchant (Type R, ~400t) — fat cargo, modest crew
Subsidized Liner / passenger packet (~600t) — passengers (ransom risk) + mixed cargo
Bulk freighter / heavy hauler (600-1000t) — rich, slow, hard to crew with a prize party
A dry hole — empty ballast run, low-value bulk, or already-looted; little to take
Armed trader or Q-ship — looks soft, hits back; a trap (escalate, expect a real fight)
Naval patrol, SDB, or customs cutter — the LAW; this is a fight or a flight, not a prize
Aslan trader or clan vessel — raiding it courts an honour vendetta (see interpretation.md)
A rival pirate / corsair working the same lane — contest, parley, or co-operate
Something strange — a derelict, a treasure-ship rumour made real, or chain a Mythic Random Event
